title: GuГӯa RГЎpida de SQL вҖ” Referencia Completa de Sintaxis

sections.heading: DDL вҖ” Lenguaje de DefiniciГіn de Datos
sections.content:
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- CREATE TABLE
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
CREATE TABLE delivery (
    id              BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    tracking_code   VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    status          VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PLANNED',
    driver_id       BIGINT REFERENCES driver(id),
    route_id        BIGINT,
    scheduled_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    delivered_at    TIMESTAMPTZ,
    notes           TEXT,
    weight_kg       NUMERIC(8,2),
    is_express      BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    CONSTRAINT fk_route FOREIGN KEY (route_id) REFERENCES route(id),
    CONSTRAINT chk_weight CHECK (weight_kg > 0)
);

-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- ALTER TABLE
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
ALTER TABLE delivery ADD COLUMN priority INT DEFAULT 0;
ALTER TABLE delivery DROP COLUMN notes;
ALTER TABLE delivery ALTER COLUMN status SET DEFAULT 'DRAFT';
ALTER TABLE delivery ALTER COLUMN status TYPE VARCHAR(30);
ALTER TABLE delivery RENAME COLUMN is_express TO express;
ALTER TABLE delivery ADD CONSTRAINT uq_tracking UNIQUE (tracking_code);
ALTER TABLE delivery DROP CONSTRAINT chk_weight;
ALTER TABLE delivery RENAME TO shipment;

-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- DROP / TRUNCATE
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS delivery CASCADE;  -- CASCADE drops dependent objects
TRUNCATE TABLE delivery RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE;  -- rГЎpido delete all filas

-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- INDEXES
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
CREATE INDEX idx_delivery_status ON delivery(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_delivery_driver_date ON delivery(driver_id, scheduled_at DESC);
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_tracking ON delivery(tracking_code);
CREATE INDEX idx_delivery_notes_gin ON delivery USING GIN (to_tsvector('english', notes));
CREATE INDEX idx_delivery_active ON delivery(scheduled_at) WHERE status = 'ACTIVE';  -- partial
CREATE INDEX idx_delivery_lower_code ON delivery(LOWER(tracking_code));  -- expression
DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_delivery_status;

-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- VIEWS
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
CREATE VIEW active_deliveries AS
  SELECT id, tracking_code, driver_id, scheduled_at
  FROM delivery WHERE status = 'ACTIVE';

CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW daily_stats AS
  SELECT DATE(scheduled_at) AS day, COUNT(*) AS total, AVG(weight_kg) AS avg_weight
  FROM delivery GROUP BY DATE(scheduled_at);

REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW daily_stats;  -- debe refresh manually
REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY daily_stats;  -- no bloqueo (needs unique Гӯndice)

-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- SEQUENCES
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
CREATE SEQUENCE delivery_id_seq START 1 INCREMENT 1;
SELECT nextval('delivery_id_seq');
SELECT currval('delivery_id_seq');
ALTER SEQUENCE delivery_id_seq RESTART WITH 1000;

sections.heading: DML вҖ” Lenguaje de ManipulaciГіn de Datos
sections.content:
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- INSERT
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
INSERT INTO delivery (tracking_code, status, driver_id, scheduled_at)
VALUES ('TRK-001', 'PLANNED', 42, '2026-07-21 08:00');

-- Insert varias filas
INSERT INTO delivery (tracking_code, status, scheduled_at) VALUES
  ('TRK-002', 'PLANNED', '2026-07-21 09:00'),
  ('TRK-003', 'PLANNED', '2026-07-21 10:00'),
  ('TRK-004', 'PLANNED', '2026-07-21 11:00');

-- Insert desde SELECT
INSERT INTO delivery_archive (id, tracking_code, status)
  SELECT id, tracking_code, status FROM delivery WHERE status = 'DELIVERED';

-- UPSERT (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT)
INSERT INTO delivery (tracking_code, status, scheduled_at)
VALUES ('TRK-001', 'DISPATCHED', NOW())
ON CONFLICT (tracking_code)
DO UPDATE SET status = EXCLUDED.status, updated_at = NOW();

INSERT INTO delivery (tracking_code, status)
VALUES ('TRK-001', 'PLANNED')
ON CONFLICT (tracking_code) DO NOTHING;  -- skip if exists

-- RETURNING (PostgreSQL) вҖ” get inserted/updated filas back
INSERT INTO delivery (tracking_code, status, scheduled_at)
VALUES ('TRK-005', 'PLANNED', NOW())
RETURNING id, tracking_code;

-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- UPDATE
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
UPDATE delivery SET status = 'DISPATCHED', updated_at = NOW()
WHERE id = 123;

UPDATE delivery SET status = 'CANCELLED'
WHERE scheduled_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days' AND status = 'PLANNED';

-- Update con subquery
UPDATE delivery d SET driver_id = (
  SELECT id FROM driver WHERE region = d.region ORDER BY workload LIMIT 1
) WHERE d.driver_id IS NULL;

-- Update con JOIN (PostgreSQL syntax)
UPDATE delivery d SET status = 'ASSIGNED'
FROM driver dr
WHERE d.driver_id = dr.id AND dr.available = true;

-- Update RETURNING
UPDATE delivery SET status = 'DISPATCHED'
WHERE id = 123 RETURNING id, status, driver_id;

-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- DELETE
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
DELETE FROM delivery WHERE id = 123;
DELETE FROM delivery WHERE status = 'CANCELLED' AND created_at < '2025-01-01';

-- Delete con subquery
DELETE FROM delivery WHERE driver_id IN (
  SELECT id FROM driver WHERE terminated = true
);

-- Delete RETURNING
DELETE FROM delivery WHERE status = 'CANCELLED' RETURNING id, tracking_code;

-- Delete in batches (evitar long bloqueos)
WITH lote AS (
  SELECT id FROM delivery WHERE status = 'ARCHIVED' LIMIT 1000
)
DELETE FROM delivery WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM lote);

sections.heading: SELECT вҖ” Consultas y Filtrado
sections.content:
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- BASIC SELECT
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
SELECT * FROM delivery;                         -- all columnas (evitar in producciГіn!)
SELECT id, status, scheduled_at FROM delivery;  -- explicit columnas
SELECT DISTINCT status FROM delivery;           -- unique valores
SELECT DISTINCT ON (driver_id) * FROM delivery  -- primero fila por driver (PostgreSQL)
  ORDER BY driver_id, scheduled_at DESC;

-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- WHERE вҖ” Filtering
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
WHERE status = 'ACTIVE'                     -- equality
WHERE status != 'CANCELLED'                 -- not equal (tambiГ©n <>)
WHERE weight_kg > 10                        -- comparison: >, <, >=, <=
WHERE weight_kg BETWEEN 5 AND 20            -- rango (inclusive)
WHERE status IN ('PLANNED', 'DISPATCHED')   -- set membership
WHERE status NOT IN ('CANCELLED', 'FAILED') -- exclusion
WHERE driver_id IS NULL                     -- null check
WHERE driver_id IS NOT NULL                 -- not null
WHERE tracking_code LIKE 'TRK-%'           -- pattern (% = any chars)
WHERE tracking_code LIKE 'TRK-00_'         -- pattern (_ = single char)
WHERE tracking_code ILIKE 'trk-%'          -- case-insensitive (PostgreSQL)
WHERE notes SIMILAR TO '%(urgent|express)%' -- regex-like (PostgreSQL)
WHERE scheduled_at >= CURRENT_DATE          -- date comparison
WHERE scheduled_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'  -- relative date

-- Compound condiciones
WHERE status = 'ACTIVE' AND driver_id IS NOT NULL
WHERE status = 'FAILED' OR (status = 'PLANNED' AND scheduled_at < NOW())
WHERE NOT (status = 'CANCELLED')

-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- ORDER BY
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
ORDER BY scheduled_at ASC                   -- ascending (predeterminado)
ORDER BY scheduled_at DESC                  -- descending
ORDER BY status ASC, scheduled_at DESC      -- multi-columna
ORDER BY CASE status                        -- custom order
  WHEN 'URGENT' THEN 1
  WHEN 'ACTIVE' THEN 2
  ELSE 3 END
ORDER BY scheduled_at NULLS LAST            -- nulls at end

-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- LIMIT & OFFSET (pagination)
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
LIMIT 50                      -- primero 50 filas
LIMIT 50 OFFSET 100           -- skip 100, devolver next 50
FETCH FIRST 10 ROWS ONLY     -- SQL standard syntax
FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY      -- mismo thing

sections.heading: JOINs
sections.content:
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- JOIN TYPES
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- INNER JOIN: only coincidentes filas
SELECT d.id, dr.name
FROM delivery d
INNER JOIN driver dr ON d.driver_id = dr.id;

-- LEFT JOIN: all desde izquierda, NULL if no match on derecha
SELECT d.id, dr.name
FROM delivery d
LEFT JOIN driver dr ON d.driver_id = dr.id;

-- RIGHT JOIN: all desde derecha (rara vez utilizado)
SELECT d.id, dr.name
FROM delivery d
RIGHT JOIN driver dr ON d.driver_id = dr.id;

-- FULL OUTER JOIN: all desde both sides
SELECT d.id, dr.name
FROM delivery d
FULL OUTER JOIN driver dr ON d.driver_id = dr.id;

-- CROSS JOIN: cartesian product (cada combo)
SELECT d.id, ts.slot
FROM delivery d
CROSS JOIN time_slot ts;

-- SELF JOIN: tabla joined to itself
SELECT d1.id, d2.id AS next_delivery
FROM delivery d1
JOIN delivery d2 ON d1.driver_id = d2.driver_id
  AND d2.scheduled_at > d1.scheduled_at;

-- LATERAL JOIN: subquery puede reference outer tabla (PostgreSQL)
SELECT d.id, latest_event.event_type
FROM delivery d
LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
  SELECT event_type FROM delivery_event e
  WHERE e.delivery_id = d.id
  ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1
) latest_event ON true;

-- Multiple JOINs
SELECT d.id, dr.name, r.region, COUNT(ds.id) AS stop_count
FROM delivery d
JOIN driver dr ON d.driver_id = dr.id
JOIN route r ON d.route_id = r.id
LEFT JOIN delivery_stop ds ON ds.delivery_id = d.id
GROUP BY d.id, dr.name, r.region;

-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- EXISTS / NOT EXISTS (correlated subquery)
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
SELECT d.* FROM delivery d
WHERE EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 FROM delivery_stop ds
  WHERE ds.delivery_id = d.id AND ds.status = 'FAILED'
);

SELECT d.* FROM delivery d
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 FROM delivery_event e
  WHERE e.delivery_id = d.id AND e.event_type = 'CONFIRMED'
);

sections.heading: AgregaciГіn вҖ” GROUP BY, HAVING y Funciones de AgregaciГіn
sections.content:
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- AGGREGATE FUNCTIONS
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
SELECT
  COUNT(*)                    AS total_rows,
  COUNT(driver_id)            AS assigned_count,   -- excludes NULL
  COUNT(DISTINCT driver_id)   AS unique_drivers,
  SUM(weight_kg)              AS total_weight,
  AVG(weight_kg)              AS avg_weight,
  MIN(scheduled_at)           AS earliest,
  MAX(scheduled_at)           AS latest,
  BOOL_OR(is_express)         AS has_any_express,  -- PostgreSQL
  ARRAY_AGG(tracking_code)    AS all_codes,        -- PostgreSQL
  STRING_AGG(status, ', ')    AS statuses          -- PostgreSQL
FROM delivery;

-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- GROUP BY
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS total
FROM delivery
GROUP BY status;

SELECT driver_id, DATE(scheduled_at) AS day, COUNT(*) AS deliveries
FROM delivery
GROUP BY driver_id, DATE(scheduled_at)
ORDER BY day, deliveries DESC;

-- GROUP BY con expression
SELECT
  CASE WHEN weight_kg < 5 THEN 'light'
       WHEN weight_kg < 20 THEN 'medium'
       ELSE 'heavy' END AS category,
  COUNT(*)
FROM delivery
GROUP BY 1;  -- reference by position

-- GROUPING SETS / ROLLUP / CUBE (advanced aggregation)
SELECT status, driver_id, COUNT(*)
FROM delivery
GROUP BY GROUPING SETS (
  (status, driver_id),  -- detail
  (status),             -- subtotal by status
  ()                    -- grand total
);

SELECT status, driver_id, COUNT(*)
FROM delivery
GROUP BY ROLLUP (status, driver_id);  -- hierarchical subtotals

-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- HAVING вҖ” filter on aggregates
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
SELECT driver_id, COUNT(*) AS total
FROM delivery
GROUP BY driver_id
HAVING COUNT(*) > 10;  -- only drivers con 10+ deliveries

SELECT status, AVG(weight_kg) AS avg_w
FROM delivery
GROUP BY status
HAVING AVG(weight_kg) > 15;

sections.heading: Funciones de Ventana
sections.content:
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- WINDOW FUNCTIONS вҖ” compute entre filas sin collapsing
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ

-- ROW_NUMBER: sequential number within particiГіn
SELECT id, driver_id, scheduled_at,
  ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY driver_id ORDER BY scheduled_at) AS seq
FROM delivery;

-- RANK: mismo rank for ties, gaps despuГ©s
SELECT id, weight_kg,
  RANK() OVER (ORDER BY weight_kg DESC) AS weight_rank
FROM delivery;

-- DENSE_RANK: mismo rank for ties, NO gaps
SELECT id, weight_kg,
  DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY weight_kg DESC) AS weight_rank
FROM delivery;

-- NTILE: divide en N equal buckets
SELECT id, weight_kg,
  NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY weight_kg) AS quartile
FROM delivery;

-- LAG / LEAD: access previous/next fila
SELECT id, scheduled_at,
  LAG(scheduled_at) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_id ORDER BY scheduled_at) AS prev_delivery,
  LEAD(scheduled_at) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_id ORDER BY scheduled_at) AS next_delivery,
  scheduled_at - LAG(scheduled_at) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_id ORDER BY scheduled_at) AS gap
FROM delivery;

-- FIRST_VALUE / LAST_VALUE / NTH_VALUE
SELECT id, driver_id, scheduled_at,
  FIRST_VALUE(id) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_id ORDER BY scheduled_at) AS first_delivery,
  LAST_VALUE(id) OVER (
    PARTITION BY driver_id ORDER BY scheduled_at
    ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
  ) AS last_delivery
FROM delivery;

-- Running totals / moving averages
SELECT id, weight_kg,
  SUM(weight_kg) OVER (ORDER BY id) AS running_total,
  AVG(weight_kg) OVER (ORDER BY id ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS moving_avg_3
FROM delivery;

-- Percent of total
SELECT driver_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (), 1) AS pct
FROM delivery GROUP BY driver_id;

-- Named WINDOW (reuse definition)
SELECT id, driver_id, scheduled_at,
  ROW_NUMBER() OVER w AS seq,
  LAG(scheduled_at) OVER w AS prev
FROM delivery
WINDOW w AS (PARTITION BY driver_id ORDER BY scheduled_at);

sections.heading: Subconsultas y CTE
sections.content:
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- SUBQUERIES
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- Scalar subquery (devuelve single valor)
SELECT id, weight_kg,
  weight_kg - (SELECT AVG(weight_kg) FROM delivery) AS diff_from_avg
FROM delivery;

-- IN subquery
SELECT * FROM delivery
WHERE driver_id IN (SELECT id FROM driver WHERE region = 'NORTH');

-- Correlated subquery (references outer consulta)
SELECT d.* FROM delivery d
WHERE d.weight_kg > (
  SELECT AVG(d2.weight_kg) FROM delivery d2
  WHERE d2.driver_id = d.driver_id  -- references outer 'd'
);

-- FROM subquery (derived tabla)
SELECT driver_id, avg_weight
FROM (
  SELECT driver_id, AVG(weight_kg) AS avg_weight
  FROM delivery GROUP BY driver_id
) sub
WHERE avg_weight > 15;

-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- CTEs (Common Table Expressions) вҖ” WITH clause
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
WITH active_drivers AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT driver_id FROM delivery WHERE status = 'ACTIVE'
),
driver_stats AS (
  SELECT driver_id, COUNT(*) AS total, AVG(weight_kg) AS avg_w
  FROM delivery
  WHERE driver_id IN (SELECT driver_id FROM active_drivers)
  GROUP BY driver_id
)
SELECT ds.*, dr.name
FROM driver_stats ds
JOIN driver dr ON ds.driver_id = dr.id
ORDER BY ds.total DESC;

-- Recursive CTE (hierarchical datos)
WITH RECURSIVE org_tree AS (
  -- Base case: top-level managers
  SELECT id, name, manager_id, 1 AS level
  FROM employee WHERE manager_id IS NULL
  UNION ALL
  -- Recursive step: employees bajo each manager
  SELECT e.id, e.name, e.manager_id, t.level + 1
  FROM employee e
  JOIN org_tree t ON e.manager_id = t.id
)
SELECT * FROM org_tree ORDER BY level, name;

-- CTE con INSERT (writable CTE вҖ” PostgreSQL)
WITH moved AS (
  DELETE FROM delivery WHERE status = 'ARCHIVED' RETURNING *
)
INSERT INTO delivery_archive SELECT * FROM moved;

sections.heading: Operaciones de Conjuntos y CASE
sections.content:
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- SET OPERATIONS
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- UNION: combine results, remove duplicates
SELECT id, tracking_code FROM delivery WHERE status = 'URGENT'
UNION
SELECT id, tracking_code FROM delivery WHERE is_express = true;

-- UNION ALL: combine results, keep duplicates (mГЎs rГЎpido!)
SELECT id, 'delivery' AS source FROM delivery
UNION ALL
SELECT id, 'devolver' AS source FROM delivery_return;

-- INTERSECT: filas in BOTH consultas
SELECT driver_id FROM delivery WHERE status = 'ACTIVE'
INTERSECT
SELECT driver_id FROM delivery WHERE is_express = true;

-- EXCEPT: filas in primero but NOT in segundo
SELECT driver_id FROM driver
EXCEPT
SELECT DISTINCT driver_id FROM delivery WHERE status = 'FAILED';

-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- CASE вҖ” conditional logic
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- Simple CASE
SELECT id,
  CASE status
    WHEN 'PLANNED' THEN 'рҹ“Ӣ Planned'
    WHEN 'DISPATCHED' THEN 'рҹҡҡ On the way'
    WHEN 'DELIVERED' THEN 'вң… Done'
    ELSE 'вқ“ Unknown'
  END AS status_label
FROM delivery;

-- Searched CASE (con condiciones)
SELECT id, weight_kg,
  CASE
    WHEN weight_kg < 5 THEN 'light'
    WHEN weight_kg < 20 THEN 'medium'
    WHEN weight_kg < 50 THEN 'heavy'
    ELSE 'oversized'
  END AS weight_category
FROM delivery;

-- CASE in WHERE/ORDER BY
SELECT * FROM delivery
ORDER BY CASE WHEN status = 'URGENT' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, scheduled_at;

-- COALESCE: primero non-NULL valor
SELECT COALESCE(driver_id, -1) AS driver_id FROM delivery;
SELECT COALESCE(notes, 'No notes') AS notes FROM delivery;

-- NULLIF: devuelve NULL if valores are equal
SELECT NULLIF(status, 'UNKNOWN') FROM delivery;  -- treat 'UNKNOWN' as NULL

-- GREATEST / LEAST
SELECT GREATEST(scheduled_at, created_at) AS latest_date FROM delivery;

sections.heading: Funciones de Fecha y Hora
sections.content:
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- DATE/TIME вҖ” PostgreSQL
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- Current timestamps
SELECT NOW();                           -- 2026-07-21 15:30:00+02
SELECT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP;               -- mismo as NOW()
SELECT CURRENT_DATE;                    -- 2026-07-21
SELECT CURRENT_TIME;                    -- 15:30:00+02
SELECT CLOCK_TIMESTAMP();               -- real clock (changes within statement)

-- Extract parts
SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM scheduled_at) FROM delivery;
SELECT EXTRACT(MONTH FROM scheduled_at) FROM delivery;
SELECT EXTRACT(DOW FROM scheduled_at) FROM delivery;   -- 0=Sun, 6=Sat
SELECT EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM scheduled_at) FROM delivery; -- unix timestamp
SELECT DATE_PART('hour', scheduled_at) FROM delivery;

-- Date arithmetic
SELECT scheduled_at + INTERVAL '2 hours' FROM delivery;
SELECT scheduled_at - INTERVAL '30 days' FROM delivery;
SELECT scheduled_at + 7 * INTERVAL '1 day' FROM delivery;
SELECT AGE(delivered_at, scheduled_at) FROM delivery;   -- interval entre
SELECT NOW() - scheduled_at AS time_since FROM delivery;

-- Truncate to precision
SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', scheduled_at) FROM delivery; -- primero of month
SELECT DATE_TRUNC('hour', scheduled_at) FROM delivery;  -- start of hour
SELECT DATE_TRUNC('week', CURRENT_DATE);                -- monday of week

-- Format
SELECT TO_CHAR(scheduled_at, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS') FROM delivery;
SELECT TO_CHAR(scheduled_at, 'Day, DD Mon YYYY') FROM delivery;

-- Parse
SELECT TO_TIMESTAMP('2026-07-21 15:30:00', 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS');
SELECT TO_DATE('21/07/2026', 'DD/MM/YYYY');

-- Generate series (Гәtil for reports con gaps)
SELECT generate_series(
  '2026-07-01'::date, '2026-07-31'::date, '1 day'::interval
) AS day;

-- Date ranges (PostgreSQL)
SELECT * FROM delivery
WHERE scheduled_at <@ tstzrange('2026-07-01', '2026-08-01');  -- contained in rango

sections.heading: Funciones de Cadenas
sections.content:
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- STRING FUNCTIONS
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
SELECT LENGTH('hello');                    -- 5
SELECT UPPER('hello');                     -- HELLO
SELECT LOWER('HELLO');                     -- hello
SELECT INITCAP('hello world');             -- Hello World
SELECT TRIM('  hello  ');                  -- 'hello'
SELECT LTRIM('  hello');                   -- 'hello'
SELECT RTRIM('hello  ');                   -- 'hello'
SELECT REPLACE('hello world', 'world', 'SQL');  -- 'hello SQL'
SELECT SUBSTRING('hello' FROM 2 FOR 3);   -- 'ell'
SELECT LEFT('hello', 3);                   -- 'hel'
SELECT RIGHT('hello', 3);                  -- 'llo'
SELECT POSITION('lo' IN 'hello');          -- 4
SELECT SPLIT_PART('a.b.c', '.', 2);       -- 'b'
SELECT REPEAT('ha', 3);                   -- 'hahaha'
SELECT REVERSE('hello');                   -- 'olleh'
SELECT LPAD('42', 5, '0');                -- '00042'
SELECT RPAD('hi', 5, '.');                -- 'hi...'

-- Concatenation
SELECT 'Hello' || ' ' || 'World';         -- 'Hello World'
SELECT CONCAT('Hello', ' ', 'World');      -- 'Hello World' (handles NULL)
SELECT CONCAT_WS(', ', 'a', 'b', 'c');   -- 'a, b, c'

-- Pattern coincidentes
SELECT 'hello' LIKE 'h%';                 -- true
SELECT 'hello' SIMILAR TO 'h(e|a)llo';   -- true (PostgreSQL regex-like)
SELECT 'hello' ~ '^h.*o$';               -- true (POSIX regex)
SELECT REGEXP_REPLACE('abc123', '[0-9]+', 'X');  -- 'abcX'
SELECT REGEXP_MATCHES('abc123def456', '[0-9]+', 'g');  -- {123}, {456}

-- Type casting
SELECT CAST('42' AS INTEGER);             -- 42
SELECT '42'::INTEGER;                     -- 42 (PostgreSQL shorthand)
SELECT 42::TEXT;                           -- '42'

sections.heading: Transacciones y Bloqueos
sections.content:
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- TRANSACTIONS
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
BEGIN;  -- or START TRANSACTION
  UPDATE delivery SET status = 'DISPATCHED' WHERE id = 123;
  INSERT INTO delivery_event (delivery_id, event_type) VALUES (123, 'DISPATCHED');
COMMIT;

BEGIN;
  UPDATE account SET balance = balance - 100 WHERE id = 1;
  UPDATE account SET balance = balance + 100 WHERE id = 2;
  -- something goes wrong:
ROLLBACK;  -- undo everything

-- SAVEPOINT (partial rollback)
BEGIN;
  INSERT INTO delivery (tracking_code) VALUES ('TRK-A');
  SAVEPOINT sp1;
  INSERT INTO delivery (tracking_code) VALUES ('TRK-B');
  ROLLBACK TO sp1;  -- undo TRK-B only
  INSERT INTO delivery (tracking_code) VALUES ('TRK-C');
COMMIT;  -- TRK-A and TRK-C are committed

-- Isolation levels
SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ COMMITTED;    -- PostgreSQL predeterminado
SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL REPEATABLE READ;
SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL SERIALIZABLE;

BEGIN ISOLATION LEVEL SERIALIZABLE;
  -- all lecturas see a consistente instantГЎnea
  -- conflictos are detected at commit
COMMIT;

-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- LOCKING
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- SELECT FOR UPDATE: bloqueo filas for modification
SELECT * FROM delivery WHERE id = 123 FOR UPDATE;

-- SKIP LOCKED: skip filas locked by other transacciones (queue pattern)
SELECT * FROM delivery
WHERE status = 'PENDING'
ORDER BY created_at
LIMIT 1
FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED;

-- NOWAIT: fail inmediatamente if fila is locked
SELECT * FROM delivery WHERE id = 123 FOR UPDATE NOWAIT;

-- Advisory bloqueos (aplicaciГіn-level)
SELECT pg_advisory_lock(12345);      -- blocking
SELECT pg_try_advisory_lock(12345);  -- non-blocking, devuelve bool
SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(12345);

sections.heading: CaracterГӯsticas EspecГӯficas de PostgreSQL
sections.content:
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- JSONB (PostgreSQL)
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- Store and consulta JSON
CREATE TABLE delivery_meta (
  delivery_id BIGINT PRIMARY KEY,
  metadata    JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'
);

INSERT INTO delivery_meta VALUES (1, '{"priority": "alto", "tags": ["express", "fragile"]}');

-- Access fields
SELECT metadata->>'priority' FROM delivery_meta;           -- text: 'alto'
SELECT metadata->'tags' FROM delivery_meta;                -- jsonb: ["express","fragile"]
SELECT metadata->'tags'->>0 FROM delivery_meta;            -- text: 'express'
SELECT metadata #>> '{tags,0}' FROM delivery_meta;         -- text: 'express' (path)

-- Filter
SELECT * FROM delivery_meta WHERE metadata->>'priority' = 'alto';
SELECT * FROM delivery_meta WHERE metadata @> '{"priority": "alto"}';
SELECT * FROM delivery_meta WHERE metadata ? 'priority';   -- clave exists
SELECT * FROM delivery_meta WHERE metadata->'tags' ? 'express'; -- array contiene

-- Update JSONB
UPDATE delivery_meta SET metadata = metadata || '{"urgent": true}';    -- merge
UPDATE delivery_meta SET metadata = metadata - 'priority';             -- remove clave
UPDATE delivery_meta SET metadata = jsonb_set(metadata, '{priority}', '"bajo"');

-- Index JSONB
CREATE INDEX idx_meta_gin ON delivery_meta USING GIN (metadata);
CREATE INDEX idx_meta_priority ON delivery_meta ((metadata->>'priority'));

-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- ARRAYS (PostgreSQL)
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
SELECT ARRAY[1, 2, 3];                    -- {1,2,3}
SELECT ARRAY_AGG(id) FROM delivery;       -- aggregate en array
SELECT UNNEST(ARRAY[1,2,3]);              -- expand array to filas
SELECT * FROM delivery WHERE 42 = ANY(stop_ids);  -- array contiene
SELECT * FROM delivery WHERE stop_ids @> ARRAY[42]; -- contiene
SELECT ARRAY_LENGTH(stop_ids, 1) FROM delivery;  -- array length

-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- PARTITIONING (PostgreSQL 10+)
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
CREATE TABLE events (
  id BIGSERIAL, event_time TIMESTAMPTZ, payload JSONB
) PARTITION BY RANGE (event_time);

CREATE TABLE events_2026_q3 PARTITION OF events
  FOR VALUES FROM ('2026-07-01') TO ('2026-10-01');

-- List particiГіn
CREATE TABLE delivery_by_region (
  id BIGSERIAL, region TEXT, datos JSONB
) PARTITION BY LIST (region);

CREATE TABLE delivery_north PARTITION OF delivery_by_region
  FOR VALUES IN ('NORTH', 'NORTHEAST');

-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- OTHER USEFUL PostgreSQL FEATURES
-- в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
-- EXPLAIN ANALYZE: show plan de ejecuciГіn con real timing
EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS, FORMAT TEXT)
  SELECT * FROM delivery WHERE status = 'ACTIVE';

-- Copy (bulk import/export)
COPY delivery FROM '/path/to/file.csv' WITH (FORMAT csv, HEADER true);
COPY (SELECT * FROM delivery WHERE status = 'ACTIVE') TO '/tmp/active.csv' CSV HEADER;

-- Generate series
SELECT * FROM generate_series(1, 10) AS n;  -- numbers 1..10
SELECT * FROM generate_series('2026-01-01'::date, '2026-12-31'::date, '1 month');

-- Conditional aggregation (pivot-like)
SELECT driver_id,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'DELIVERED') AS delivered,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'FAILED') AS failed,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'ACTIVE') AS active
FROM delivery GROUP BY driver_id;

title: JOINs SQL y Patrones de Consulta вҖ” De lo BГЎsico a ProducciГіn

sections.heading: Tipos de JOIN Visualizados
sections.content:
```
INNER JOIN:  Solo las filas coincidentes en AMBAS tablas
LEFT JOIN:   Todas las filas de la IZQUIERDA mГЎs las coincidentes de la derecha (NULL si no hay coincidencia)
RIGHT JOIN:  Todas las filas de la DERECHA mГЎs las coincidentes de la izquierda (se usa rara vez)
FULL JOIN:   Todas las filas de AMBAS tablas (NULL donde no hay coincidencia)
CROSS JOIN:  Producto cartesiano (cada fila Г— cada fila)
```

```sql
-- INNER: only orders WITH a customer
SELECT o.*, c.name
FROM orders o
INNER JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id;

-- LEFT: ALL orders, even if customer was deleted
SELECT o.*, c.name
FROM orders o
LEFT JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id;
-- c.name = NULL cuando customer doesn't exist
```

sections.heading: Self-JOIN para JerarquГӯas
sections.content:
When a tabla references itself (employee вҶ’ manager, category вҶ’ parent_category):

```sql
-- Buscar employees and their managers
SELECT e.name AS employee, m.name AS manager
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN employee m ON e.manager_id = m.id;

-- Recursive CTE for full hierarchy (PostgreSQL)
WITH RECURSIVE ГЎrbol AS (
    SELECT id, name, parent_id, 0 AS depth
    FROM category WHERE parent_id IS NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT c.id, c.name, c.parent_id, t.depth + 1
    FROM category c
    JOIN ГЎrbol t ON c.parent_id = t.id
)
SELECT * FROM ГЎrbol ORDER BY depth, name;
```

sections.heading: LATERAL JOIN вҖ” Subconsulta Correlacionada como JOIN
sections.content:
PostgreSQL-specific but extremely powerful. Like a subquery that puede reference the outer tabla:

```sql
-- Top 3 comments por post (LATERAL)
SELECT p.title, c.review, c.score
FROM post p
CROSS JOIN LATERAL (
    SELECT review, score
    FROM post_comment
    WHERE post_id = p.id
    ORDER BY score DESC
    LIMIT 3
) c;
```

Equivalent to a correlated subquery but puede devolver varias columnas/filas. Much mГЎs readable than window function + filter approaches.

sections.heading: Patrones Anti-JOIN
sections.content:
Buscar filas que NO tienen coincidencia:

```sql
-- вң… NOT EXISTS (normalmente la opciГіn mГЎs rГЎpida: se detiene en la primera coincidencia)
SELECT p.*
FROM post p
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM post_comment c WHERE c.post_id = p.id
);

-- вң… LEFT JOIN + IS NULL (tambiГ©n es rГЎpido con una indexaciГіn adecuada)
SELECT p.*
FROM post p
LEFT JOIN post_comment c ON c.post_id = p.id
WHERE c.id IS NULL;

-- вқҢ NOT IN (peligroso con valores NULL: devuelve vacГӯo si la subconsulta contiene NULL!)
SELECT * FROM post
WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT post_id FROM post_comment);  -- BUG if any post_id is NULL
```

sections.heading: Patrones de AgregaciГіn con GROUP BY
sections.content:
```sql
-- Count comments por post
SELECT p.id, p.title, COUNT(c.id) AS comment_count
FROM post p
LEFT JOIN post_comment c ON c.post_id = p.id
GROUP BY p.id, p.title
HAVING COUNT(c.id) > 5
ORDER BY comment_count DESC;

-- Conditional aggregation (pivot-like)
SELECT
    date_trunc('month', created_on) AS month,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'PENDING') AS pending,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'SHIPPED') AS shipped,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'DELIVERED') AS delivered
FROM orders
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1;
```

`FILTER (WHERE ...)` is PostgreSQL-specific but very clean for conditional counts.

title: ГҚndices y EXPLAIN вҖ” CГіmo Acelerar Consultas

sections.heading: CГіmo Funcionan los ГҚndices B-Tree
sections.content:
A B-Tree Гӯndice is a ordenado, balanced ГЎrbol structure donde:
- **Leaf nodos** contener pointers to real tabla filas (heap tuples)
- **Internal nodos** guide the search (like a phone book)
- **O(log n) bГәsquedas** вҖ” finding a fila in 1 billion registros takes ~30 pГЎgina lecturas

```sql
CREATE INDEX idx_post_created ON post(created_on);

-- This consulta puede use the Гӯndice:
SELECT * FROM post WHERE created_on > '2024-01-01';

-- The Гӯndice stores created_on valores SORTED, so rango escaneos are rГЎpido
```

**Sin Гӯndice:** Escaneo completo de la tabla вҖ” leer cada single fila (O(n)).
**Con Гӯndice:** Recorrido del B-Tree вҖ” jump directamente to coincidentes filas (O(log n)).

sections.heading: ГҚndices Compuestos вҖ” El Orden de las Columnas Importa
sections.content:
```sql
CREATE INDEX idx_order_status_date ON orders(status, created_on);
```

**Regla del prefijo mГЎs a la izquierda:** The Гӯndice is Гәtil for consultas that filter on:
- `status` alone вң…
- `status` AND `created_on` вң…
- `created_on` alone вқҢ (Гӯndice not utilizado!)

**HeurГӯstica del orden:** Put primero las condiciones de igualdad y al final las condiciones de rango:
```sql
-- вң… Optimal: equality (status) primero, rango (created_on) segundo
WHERE status = 'ACTIVE' AND created_on > '2024-01-01'

-- Index: (status, created_on) вҶ’ seeks to 'ACTIVE', then escaneos rango
```

sections.heading: ГҚndices de Cobertura (Index-Solo Scan)
sections.content:
If the Гӯndice contiene ALL columnas necesario by the consulta, the base de datos nunca lecturas the tabla:

```sql
CREATE INDEX idx_order_covering
ON orders(status, created_on)
INCLUDE (total, customer_id);  -- PostgreSQL INCLUDE syntax

-- This consulta is satisfied entirely desde the Гӯndice:
SELECT total, customer_id
FROM orders
WHERE status = 'SHIPPED' AND created_on > '2024-06-01';
-- Index-only escaneo: no heap access necesario!
```

Covering Гӯndices trade disco space for leer speed. Best for frequently-run analytical consultas.

sections.heading: EXPLAIN ANALYZE вҖ” InterpretaciГіn de Planes de EjecuciГіn
sections.content:
```sql
EXPLAIN ANALYZE
SELECT * FROM orders WHERE customer_id = 42 AND status = 'ACTIVE';
```

Aspectos clave que hay que revisar:

| Node | Meaning | Performance |
|------|---------|:-----------:|
| Seq Scan | Escaneo completo de la tabla | рҹ”ҙ Bad (on grande tablas) |
| Index Scan | B-Tree bГәsqueda + heap fetch | рҹҹў Good |
| Index Solo Scan | Answered desde Гӯndice alone | рҹҹў Best |
| Bitmap Index Scan | Index вҶ’ bitmap вҶ’ heap | рҹҹЎ OK (muchos filas) |
| Nested Loop | For each outer fila, escaneo inner | рҹ”ҙ Bad if inner has no Гӯndice |
| Hash Join | Build hash tabla, probe | рҹҹў Adecuado para grande joins |

**Tiempo real frente al estimado:** Always use `ANALYZE` to see real execution times. Estimates puede be wildly wrong if statistics are stale (`ANALYZE table_name` to refresh).

sections.heading: ГҚndices Parciales y de ExpresiГіn
sections.content:
**ГҚndice parcial:** Solo Гӯndice filas that match a condiciГіn:
```sql
-- Solo Гӯndice active orders (skip 90% of historical datos)
CREATE INDEX idx_active_orders ON orders(created_on)
WHERE status = 'ACTIVE';
```
Much smaller Гӯndice вҶ’ mГЎs rГЎpido bГәsquedas, menos disco, menos maintenance.

**ГҚndice de expresiГіn:** Index a computed valor:
```sql
-- Case-insensitive search
CREATE INDEX idx_email_lower ON users(LOWER(email));
SELECT * FROM users WHERE LOWER(email) = 'user@example.com';

-- JSONB field
CREATE INDEX idx_meta_type ON products((metadata->>'type'));
```

title: Transacciones y ACID вҖ” CorrecciГіn bajo Concurrencia

sections.heading: Propiedades ACID
sections.content:
| Property | Meaning | Mechanism |
|----------|---------|----------|
| **Atomicity** | Todo o nada: un fallo parcial provoca una reversiГіn | Undo log (rollback segment) |
| **Consistency** | La base de datos pasa de un estado vГЎlido a otro | Constraints, triggers, app logic |
| **Isolation** | Las transacciones concurrentes no interfieren entre sГӯ | MVCC + bloqueos |
| **Durability** | Los datos confirmados sobreviven a fallos | WAL (Write-Ahead Log) |

**En la prГЎctica:** Atomicity and Durability are handled by the base de datos motor. Consistency is mostly your responsibility (correcto constraints + aplicaciГіn logic). Isolation is donde 90% of bugs hide.

sections.heading: Niveles de Aislamiento en la PrГЎctica
sections.content:
| Level | Dirty Read | Non-Repeatable Read | Phantom | Performance |
|-------|:----------:|:-------------------:|:-------:|:-----------:|
| READ UNCOMMITTED | вң… allowed | вң… allowed | вң… allowed | Fastest |
| READ COMMITTED | вқҢ blocked | вң… allowed | вң… allowed | Default (PG, Oracle) |
| REPEATABLE READ | вқҢ blocked | вқҢ blocked | вң… allowed* | Moderate |
| SERIALIZABLE | вқҢ blocked | вқҢ blocked | вқҢ blocked | Slowest |

*PostgreSQL's REPEATABLE READ actually prevents phantoms (true instantГЎnea aislamiento).

**Valor predeterminado en PostgreSQL:** READ COMMITTED вҖ” each SQL statement sees a instantГЎnea nueva of committed datos.
**Valor predeterminado en MySQL InnoDB:** REPEATABLE READ вҖ” entire transacciГіn sees one instantГЎnea.

sections.heading: Bloqueos вҖ” Pesimista vs Optimista
sections.content:
**Bloqueo pesimista** вҖ” block other transacciones:
```sql
-- Exclusive bloqueo: blocks all other SELECTs FOR UPDATE and UPDATEs
SELECT * FROM account WHERE id = 1 FOR UPDATE;
UPDATE account SET balance = balance - 100 WHERE id = 1;
COMMIT;
```

**Bloqueo optimista** вҖ” detect conflictos despuГ©s the fact:
```sql
-- Read version
SELECT id, balance, version FROM account WHERE id = 1;
-- (balance=500, version=3)

-- Update only if version matches
UPDATE account SET balance = 400, version = 4
WHERE id = 1 AND version = 3;
-- If rows_affected = 0 вҶ’ conflicto detected вҶ’ reintentar or fail
```

**CuГЎndo utilizar cada uno:**
- Web app con bajo conflicto вҶ’ Optimistic (JPA @Version)
- Financial transfer, inventory decrement вҶ’ Pessimistic (SELECT FOR UPDATE)
- Batch job modifying muchos filas вҶ’ Advisory bloqueos or queue-based

sections.heading: Interbloqueos
sections.content:
Deadlock: Transaction A holds bloqueo on fila 1, waits for fila 2. Transaction B holds bloqueo on fila 2, waits for fila 1. Neither puede proceed.

```
T1: UPDATE account SET ... WHERE id = 1;  -- bloqueos fila 1
T2: UPDATE account SET ... WHERE id = 2;  -- bloqueos fila 2
T1: UPDATE account SET ... WHERE id = 2;  -- WAITS for T2
T2: UPDATE account SET ... WHERE id = 1;  -- WAITS for T1 вҶ’ DEADLOCK!
```

**Prevention:**
1. Always acquire bloqueos in the **mismo orden** (e.g., by ascending ID)
2. Keep transacciones **short** (menos time holding bloqueos)
3. Use **bloqueo timeouts** (`SET lock_timeout = '5s'`)
4. Design con **queues** en su lugar of competing for the mismo filas

PostgreSQL automГЎticamente detects deadlocks and kills one transacciГіn con an error. Your app debe be ready to reintentar.

sections.heading: Patrones de Transacciones en Spring/JPA

title: Rendimiento SQL вҖ” El Problema N+1 y la OptimizaciГіn

sections.heading: El problema de consultas N+1
Lista de comprobaciГіn para optimizar consultas
sections.content:
Optimize by evidence, not by folklore. The Гәtil order is: measure the lento solicitud, inspect its plan, make one change, then measure again con representative datos. This is mГЎs reliable than adding an Гӯndice or rewriting a consulta porque it "looks mГЎs rГЎpido". 1. Return and process menos datos: 
-- вқҢ Reads wide filas and transfers columnas the screen nunca uses
SELECT * FROM delivery WHERE status = 'PLANNED';

-- вң“ Small projection, stable order and a bounded result
SELECT id, status, driver_id, scheduled_at
FROM delivery
WHERE status = 'PLANNED'
ORDER BY scheduled_at, id
LIMIT 50;
Use a DTO projection in JPA cuando the screen is leer-only. `LIMIT` is not a substitute for pagination, but cada user-facing list needs a deliberate bound. 2. Keep predicates sargable: an indexed columna debe be searchable sin applying a function or implicit cast to cada fila. 
-- вқҢ Function wraps the indexed columna
WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM scheduled_at) = 2026

-- вң“ Range predicate puede use an Гӯndice on scheduled_at
WHERE scheduled_at >= TIMESTAMPTZ '2026-01-01'
  AND scheduled_at <  TIMESTAMPTZ '2027-01-01'
The mismo principle applies to `LOWER(email)`, leading-wildcard `LIKE '%term'`, mismatched datos types and arithmetic on a columna. If the expression is genuinely required, consider an expression Гӯndice вҖ” despuГ©s measuring. 3. Design an Гӯndice for the access path: start desde the frequent `WHERE`, `JOIN`, `ORDER BY` and selected columnas together. For a list filtered by status and ordered by date, `(status, scheduled_at, id)` is a plausible candidate; its leftmost columnas matter. Indexing cada columna or cada bajo-cardinality valor simply makes escrituras and maintenance mГЎs costoso. 4. Treat EXISTS, IN, JOIN and CTEs as semantic choices primero: `EXISTS` expresses "does a related fila exist?" and avoids duplicate parent filas. `JOIN` is derecha cuando columnas desde both relations are necesario. PostgreSQL puede optimize `IN` and a non-materialized CTE well, so do not assume one is siempre mГЎs rГЎpido вҖ” inspect the plan. A CTE is often a readability herramienta; `MATERIALIZED` deliberately changes that trade-off.

sections.heading: Lee el plan antes de cambiar el SQL
sections.content:
EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS)
SELECT d.id, d.status, d.scheduled_at
FROM delivery d
WHERE d.status = 'PLANNED'
  AND d.scheduled_at >= now() - interval '7 days'
ORDER BY d.scheduled_at, d.id
LIMIT 50;
Read a PostgreSQL plan in this order: вҖў Actual time and loops: buscar the nodo doing the real work, not merely the nodo at the top. A cheap operation repeated thousands of times is costoso. вҖў Estimated filas vs real filas: a grande difference normalmente points to estadГӯsticas desactualizadas, correlated columnas or a poor predicate estimate. Run `ANALYZE` donde appropriate; only then consider extended statistics or a consulta/Гӯndice change. вҖў Access path: a sequential escaneo is not automГЎticamente malo вҖ” it is often correcto for a pequeГұo tabla or a consulta returning most filas. It becomes suspicious cuando it lecturas a grande tabla to devolver a tiny fraction. вҖў Sort, hash and temporary I/O: external disco sorts, grande hash batches and repeated nested-loop inner escaneos are concrete leads. Check filters, join Гӯndices and fila counts antes forcing a plan. вҖў Buffers: shared hits versus lecturas tell you whether the issue is CPU/planning or almacenamiento I/O. `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` executes the statement. Use it carefully con escrituras and producciГіn datos; prefer a transacciГіn that you puede roll back for investigative DML.

sections.heading: JOINs, paginaciГіn y trabajo repetido
sections.content:
Joins: Гӯndice join claves on the grande/inner side, filter early cuando it reduces filas, and project only quГ© is necesario. Do not manually reorder joins to outsmart PostgreSQL sin evidence; the planner puede reorder muchos joins, while estadГӯsticas desactualizadas puede make it elegir poorly. Pagination: paginaciГіn por desplazamiento is sencillo and supports jumping to a pГЎgina, but later offsets still require the base de datos to buscar and discard earlier filas. Cursor/paginaciГіn por clave is normalmente the better predeterminado for a "next pГЎgina" feed. 
-- Keyset pagination: stable, bounded work por next pГЎgina
SELECT id, status, scheduled_at
FROM delivery
WHERE (scheduled_at, id) > (:lastScheduledAt, :lastId)
ORDER BY scheduled_at, id
LIMIT 50;
The ordering debe be deterministic and supported by an Гӯndice such as `(scheduled_at, id)`. Sorting and aggregation: `ORDER BY`, `GROUP BY`, `DISTINCT` and `UNION` puede require substantial memoria, disco or deduplication work. Remove them cuando the product does not necesitar them; use `UNION ALL` only cuando duplicates are valid. For costoso, repeated reporting aggregates, evaluate a materialized view or a pre-aggregation pipeline and define its refresh/freshness contract. Production guardrails: set a sensible statement timeout, observe lento-consulta metrics, keep statistics real, and verify the improvement at producciГіn-like cardinality. An Гӯndice that speeds up a leer puede increase latencia de escritura, vacuum work and almacenamiento вҖ” that trade-off belongs in the decision.

sections.heading: Perspectiva SГ©nior
sections.content:
Senior engineers don't just escritura SQL вҖ” they leer plan de ejecuciГіns, understand the planificador de consultas's decisions, and design schemas for their access patterns.

Key distinctions:
вҖў An Гӯndice that improves one consulta puede degrade escrituras entre the tabla
вҖў EXPLAIN ANALYZE is the single most importante debugging herramienta
вҖў The difference entre a 100ms consulta and a 10s consulta is often one missing composite Гӯndice
вҖў Isolation levels are a trade-off entre correctness and throughput, not a predeterminado to set and forget
вҖў Connection pooling, statement timeouts and bloqueo monitoring are producciГіn essentials

sections.heading: Perspectiva REWE
sections.content:
In TRAB's delivery base de datos:

вҖў Route planning consultas join deliveries Г— stops Г— drivers Г— time windows
вҖў Indexes debe support both real-time bГәsquedas (by driver, by route) and lote reporting (by date rango, by warehouse)
вҖў PostgreSQL's MVCC means long-running reports don't block transactional inserts вҖ” but they puede bloat the tabla
вҖў Partitioning delivery_events by month keeps recent datos rГЎpido while archiving history
вҖў The N+1 problema appears cuando carga route details for a list of deliveries вҖ” solved con JOIN FETCH or lote carga

sections.heading: CuГЎndo NO utilizarlo
sections.content:
When NOT to optimize SQL prematurely:

вҖў Don't add Гӯndices antes measuring вҖ” each Гӯndice costs escritura rendimiento and almacenamiento
вҖў Don't denormalize antes proving the JOIN is actually the bottleneck
вҖў No lo utilices base de datos-level caching cuando aplicaciГіn-level caching (Redis) is mГЎs appropriate
вҖў No lo utilices SERIALIZABLE aislamiento unless you puede prove the anomaly you're preventing
вҖў No lo utilices stored procedures for business logic that changes frequently

title: Motores de Almacenamiento вҖ” B-Trees vs LSM-Trees (DDIA Cap. 3)

sections.heading: Por quГ© los motores de almacenamiento importan
sections.content: You won't implement a almacenamiento motor, but you debe elegir one appropriate for your workload. The two dominant families: вҖў B-Trees (PostgreSQL, MySQL InnoDB, Oracle) вҖ” pГЎgina-oriented, in-place updates вҖў LSM-Trees (Cassandra, RocksDB, LevelDB, HBase) вҖ” log-structured, solo anexado, compaction The choice affects leer/escritura rendimiento, amplificaciГіn de espacio, and compaction overhead. Understanding the internals lets you predict behavior bajo load and tune accordingly. Idea clave desde Kleppmann: "Well-chosen Гӯndices speed up leer consultas, but cada Гӯndice slows down escrituras. Databases don't Гӯndice everything by predeterminado вҖ” you elegir Гӯndices using your knowledge of the aplicaciГіn's consulta patterns."

sections.heading: B-Trees вҖ” la base de las bases de datos relacionales
sections.content:
CГіmo funcionan los B-Trees: вҖў Data organized in fixed-size pГЎginas (normalmente 4KB) вҖў Each pГЎgina puede be identified by an address (like a pointer on disco) вҖў Pages form a ГЎrbol: raГӯz вҶ’ internal nodos вҶ’ hoja nodos containing real valores вҖў Lookups traverse desde raГӯz to hoja: O(log n) вҖ” normalmente 3-4 levels for millions of filas вҖў Branching factor ~500: a 4-level ГЎrbol puede store up to 256 TB 
-- B-ГЎrbol bГәsqueda for: WHERE id = 42
Root pГЎgina вҶ’ "id entre 1-100 is in pГЎgina 3"
  Page 3 вҶ’ "id entre 40-60 is in pГЎgina 17"
    Page 17 вҶ’ found fila con id=42 (hoja pГЎgina)
-- Solo 3 disco lecturas for millions of filas!
Write operations: вҖў Buscar the hoja pГЎgina containing the clave вҖў Overwrite the pГЎgina in place con the nuevo valor вҖў If pГЎgina is full вҶ’ split en two half-full pГЎginas, update parent вҖў Uses Write-Ahead Log (WAL) for crash recovery вҖ” cada modification written to solo anexado log BEFORE updating ГЎrbol pГЎginas Ventajas: вҖў Excellent rendimiento de lectura (O(log n)) вҖў Predictable latency вҖў Efficient consultas por rango (hoja pГЎginas linked) вҖў Mature, well-understood, decades of optimization Desventajas: вҖў Write amplification: one logical escritura = WAL escritura + pГЎgina escritura (possibly + pГЎgina split) вҖў Fragmentation sobre time вҖў Concurrent access requires careful locking (latches on pГЎginas)

sections.heading: LSM-Trees вҖ” optimizados para cargas de escritura
sections.content:
La idea principal: nunca modify datos in place. Todas escrituras go to an in-memoria balanced ГЎrbol (memtable). When memtable is full, flush to disco as a ordenado file (SSTable). SSTable (Sorted String Table): вҖў Key-valor pairs ordenado by clave вҖў Immutable once written вҖў Merging is efficient (merge-sort of ordenado files) вҖў Sparse Гӯndice in memoria (one entry por pocos KB) 
Ruta de escritura:
1. Write to in-memoria memtable (Red-Black ГЎrbol / AVL)
2. When memtable reaches threshold (~MB) вҶ’ flush to disco as SSTable
3. Background compaction: merge SSTables, discard antiguo versions

Ruta de lectura:
1. Check memtable
2. Check most recent SSTable on disco
3. Check next SSTable... (use Bloom filters to skip)
Estrategias de compactaciГіn: вҖў Size-tiered (Cassandra predeterminado): merge SSTables of similar size вҶ’ bueno rendimiento de escritura, higher amplificaciГіn de espacio вҖў Leveled (RocksDB, LevelDB): organized in levels of increasing size вҶ’ better rendimiento de lectura, mГЎs compaction I/O Ventajas: вҖў Sequential escrituras only вҶ’ much mГЎs rГЎpido on HDD and SSD (10x-100x rendimiento de escritura vs B-ГЎrbol) вҖў Better compression (ordenado datos compresses well) вҖў No fragmentation (compaction produces contiguous files) Desventajas: вҖў Reads slower (debe check varias SSTables) вҖў Compaction puede interfere con ongoing lecturas/escrituras (I/O bandwidth) вҖў Unpredictable latencia de cola durante compaction вҖў Space amplification: antiguo versions exist until compacted away

sections.heading: Matriz de decisiГіn: cuГЎndo elegir cada uno
sections.content: Elige B-Tree (PostgreSQL, MySQL) cuando: вҖў Read-heavy workload (OLTP con muchos bГәsquedas) вҖў Need predictable latency (p99 matters) вҖў Range consultas are habitual вҖў Transactions con aislamiento fuerte required вҖў Data fits in memoria or conjunto de trabajo is hot Elige LSM-Tree (Cassandra, RocksDB) cuando: вҖў Write-heavy workload (event logging, time-series, IoT) вҖў Write throughput is the bottleneck вҖў Can tolerate slightly higher latencia de lectura вҖў Data is append-mostly (logs, events, metrics) вҖў Need better space efficiency Para un sistema de entregas de REWE: вҖў Base de datos OLTP principal (orders, drivers, routes): B-Tree (PostgreSQL) вҖ” lecturas dominate, transacciones crГӯtico вҖў AlmacГ©n de eventos (GPS pings, delivery events, telemetry): LSM-Tree вҖ” escritura-heavy, solo anexado, consistencia eventual OK вҖў ГҚndice de bГәsqueda (Elasticsearch uses LSM segments internamente) Idea clave para la entrevista: "The choice entre B-Tree and LSM isn't about which is 'better' вҖ” it's about coincidentes the almacenamiento motor to your access pattern. B-Trees optimize for lecturas; LSM-Trees optimize for escrituras. Most producciГіn systems use both for diferente datos."

sections.heading: OLTP vs OLAP вҖ” procesamiento transaccional frente a analГӯtica
sections.content:
DDIA makes a crucial distinction entre two almacenamiento patterns: 
                OLTP (fila-oriented)          OLAP (columna-oriented)
Read pattern    Small number of registros       Aggregate sobre muchos filas
                by clave (bГәsquedas puntuales)        (escaneo grande datasets)
Write pattern   Random-access, bajo-latency    Bulk import (ETL) or event
                user-driven inserts           streaming
Used by         End users via web app         Internal analysts, BI tools
Data size       GB to bajo TB                  TB to PB
Bottleneck      Disk seek time                Disk bandwidth
Example         PostgreSQL, MySQL              BigQuery, Redshift, Clickhouse
Almacenamiento orientado a columnas (OLAP): вҖў Stores all valores of a columna together (not all columnas of a fila) вҖў Enables incredible compression (run-length encoding, bitmap encoding) вҖў Reads only the columnas necesario for the consulta вҖў Vectorized processing: CPU operates on compressed columna vectors 
-- This consulta in columna store lecturas ONLY status and scheduled_at columnas:
SELECT status, COUNT(*) FROM delivery
WHERE scheduled_at >= '2026-01-01'
GROUP BY status;
-- In fila store: lecturas ENTIRE filas (40 columnas) just to use 2
Vistas materializadas y cubos de datos: Pre-computed aggregates for habitual consultas. Trade almacenamiento and escritura cost for instant lecturas. DDIA calls this "aggregation: datos cubes and materialized views."

title: ReplicaciГіn вҖ” Mantener los Datos Consistentes entre MГЎquinas

sections.heading: ВҝPor quГ© replicar?
sections.content: Tres motivos para mantener copias de los datos en varias mГЎquinas: 1. Alta disponibilidad вҖ” if one nodo dies, others continue serving 2. Latency вҖ” serve users desde geographically close rГ©plicas 3. Escalabilidad de lectura вҖ” distribute leer load entre rГ©plicas All difficulty in replication lies in handling changes. If datos nunca changed, you'd copy it once and be done. The challenge is keeping rГ©plicas consistente as escrituras happen. Tres enfoques principales: вҖў Basado en lГӯder (lГӯder Гәnico) вҖў MultilГӯder вҖў Sin lГӯder

sections.heading: ReplicaciГіn LГӯder-Seguidor (un solo lГӯder)
sections.content:
El enfoque mГЎs habitual (PostgreSQL, MySQL, Oracle, MongoDB): 
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    escrituras    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  Client в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҖвҶ’в”Ӯ  Leader в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ             в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                             в”Ӯ replication stream
                    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                    в–ј        в–ј        в–ј
              в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
              в”ӮFoll.1в”Ӯ в”ӮFoll.2в”Ӯ в”ӮFoll.3в”Ӯ
              в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                  в–І        в–І        в–І
              в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
              в”Ӯ    Clients leer desde     в”Ӯ
              в”Ӯ    lГӯder OR seguidores   в”Ӯ
              в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
Rules: вҖў Solo lГӯder accepts escrituras вҖў Followers receive replication log and apply changes in mismo orden вҖў Reads puede go to any rГ©plica (lГӯder or seguidor) ReplicaciГіn sГӯncrona frente a asГӯncrona: вҖў SГӯncrona: lГӯder waits for seguidor to confirm вҶ’ guaranteed up-to-date, but any seguidor outage blocks escrituras вҖў AsГӯncrona: lГӯder doesn't wait вҶ’ rГЎpido escrituras, but seguidor puede lag (datos loss if lГӯder crashes) вҖў SemisГӯncrona (practical): one seguidor sync + others async PostgreSQL streaming replication: 
-- Primary sends WAL registros to standby in real-time
-- Standby applies WAL and puede serve leer consultas
-- Typical lag: milliseconds to seconds
-- pg_stat_replication shows lag por rГ©plica

sections.heading: Retraso de replicaciГіn вҖ” problemas de consistencia
sections.content:
When using async replication, seguidores puede be seconds behind the lГӯder. This creates observable anomalies: 1. Lectura de tus propias escrituras (leer-despuГ©s-escritura consistency): 
-- User updates their profile (goes to lГӯder)
UPDATE user_profile SET name = 'Alice' WHERE id = 42;

-- User inmediatamente refreshes pГЎgina (lecturas desde seguidor)
SELECT name FROM user_profile WHERE id = 42;
-- May still devolver antiguo name if seguidor hasn't caught up!
Solutions: вҖў Read user's own datos desde lГӯder вҖў Track escritura timestamp, route lecturas to rГ©plica that's caught up вҖў Client remembers its last escritura timestamp 2. Lecturas monotГіnicas: User sees datos, refreshes, sees OLDER datos (diferente seguidor, further behind). Solution: pin user to one rГ©plica (session affinity). 3. Lecturas con prefijo consistente: Causal order violated вҖ” seeing an answer antes the question. Solution: escritura causally related datos to mismo particiГіn. Interview answer framework: "Replication lag is an unavoidable consequence of asynchronous replication. The aplicaciГіn debe be designed to tolerate it вҖ” either by enrutamiento specific lecturas to the lГӯder, or by building the UI to handle consistencia eventual gracefully."

sections.heading: ReplicaciГіn Multi-LГӯder
sections.content:
When: Multiple datacenters, each con its own lГӯder. Writes accepted at any datacenter. 
DC Europe          DC US-East         DC Asia
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ       в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ       в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ Leader  в”Ӯв—„в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ Leader  в”Ӯв—„в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ Leader  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ+Followersв”Ӯ      в”Ӯ+Followersв”Ӯ      в”Ӯ+Followersв”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ       в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ       в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   escriturasвҶ‘           escriturasвҶ‘           escriturasвҶ‘
Ventajas: вҖў Tolerates entire datacenter outage вҖў Lower latencia de escritura (local lГӯder) вҖў Network interruptions entre DCs don't block local escrituras The BIG problema: escritura conflictos 
-- User edits title simultГЎneamente in two DCs
DC-Europe: UPDATE doc SET title = 'Title A' WHERE id = 1;
DC-US:     UPDATE doc SET title = 'Title B' WHERE id = 1;
-- Both succeed locally. Which one wins cuando they replicate?
Estrategias de resoluciГіn de conflictos: вҖў La Гәltima escritura gana (LWW): use timestamp вҶ’ sencillo but loses datos (Cassandra predeterminado) вҖў Combinar valores: keep both and let aplicaciГіn resolve (CRDTs) вҖў LГіgica personalizada: aplicaciГіn-specific handlers вҖў PrevenciГіn de conflictos: route all escrituras for a record to mismo lГӯder Usos prГЎcticos: collaborative editing (Google Docs), mobile offline-primero apps, cross-region deployment.

sections.heading: ReplicaciГіn sin LГӯder (estilo Dynamo)
sections.content:
No lГӯder at all. Client escrituras to varias rГ©plicas directamente (Cassandra, Riak, DynamoDB). 
Write: send to ALL rГ©plicas, succeed if W out of N acknowledge
Read:  leer desde ALL rГ©plicas, succeed if R out of N respond

Quorum condiciГіn: W + R > N guarantees overlap
-- With N=3, W=2, R=2: siempre leer at least one up-to-date copy
Example con N=3, W=2, R=2: 
Client escrituras valor v2 to all 3 rГ©plicas:
  Replica A: вң“ (has v2)
  Replica B: вң“ (has v2)  вҶ’ W=2 met, escritura succeeds
  Replica C: вң— (still has v1, was temporarily down)

Client lecturas desde all 3:
  Replica A: devuelve v2
  Replica B: devuelve v2  вҶ’ R=2 met, cliente sees v2 вң“
  Replica C: devuelve v1 (stale)
Repair mechanisms: вҖў Read repair: cliente detects stale valor durante leer, escrituras back to stale rГ©plica вҖў Anti-entropy: background process comparing rГ©plicas and copying missing datos Limitations of quorums: Even con W + R > N, edge cases puede devolver stale lecturas: вҖў Sloppy quorums (escrituras go to nodos outside the designated N) вҖў Concurrent escrituras (which one wins?) вҖў Write concurrent con leer (puede see antiguo or nuevo) вҖў Partial failures durante escrituras CuГЎndo utilizarlo: Alta disponibilidad requirements, tolerate consistencia eventual, multi-datacenter sin a single lГӯder. Not ideal for strong consistency needs (banking, inventory).

sections.heading: Perspectiva sГ©nior вҖ” elegir una estrategia de replicaciГіn
sections.content: Decision framework for interview: | Requirement | Strategy | |---|---| | Strong consistency, single region | Single lГӯder (PostgreSQL streaming) | | Alta disponibilidad, tolerate lag | Single lГӯder + async seguidores | | Multi-region escrituras | MultilГӯder (accept conflicto complexity) | | Maximum availability, consistencia eventual OK | Sin lГӯder (Cassandra/DynamoDB) | | Escalabilidad de lectura | Add leer rГ©plicas to any estrategia | Key Kleppmann quotes for interviews: вҖў "If the lГӯder fails and is not recoverable, any escrituras that have not yet been replicated to seguidores are lost." вҖў "Pretending that replication is synchronous cuando in fact it is asynchronous is a recipe for problems down the line." вҖў "There is no general soluciГіn to conflicto resolution... the aplicaciГіn developer debe resolve conflictos in a way that makes sense for the business logic." REWE aplicaciГіn: вҖў Delivery tracking base de datos: lГӯder Гәnico PostgreSQL con streaming replication (strong consistency for order estado) вҖў Analytics/reporting: leer rГ©plicas in each region вҖў Driver GPS events: could use leaderless for maximum availability (stale position is better than no position)

title: Particionamiento y sharding вҖ” Escalar mГЎs allГЎ de una sola mГЎquina

sections.heading: ВҝPor quГ© particionar los datos?
sections.content:
When datos grows too grande for one machine, split it en particiones (tambiГ©n called shards, regions, tablets, vnodes). Each particiГіn is a pequeГұo base de datos of its own вҖ” lives on one nodo (con replication for fault tolerance). 
Before: 1 nodo holds 10 TB
After:  10 nodos hold 1 TB each (partitioned)

         в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
         в”Ӯ   Query Router / Client      в”Ӯ
         в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
               в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ
          в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
          в”ӮPart Aв”Ӯ в”ӮPart Bв”Ӯ в”ӮPart Cв”Ӯ
          в”ӮA-H   в”Ӯ в”ӮI-P   в”Ӯ в”ӮQ-Z   в”Ӯ
          в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
Goal: spread datos and consulta load evenly. Avoid hot spots (one particiГіn getting disproportionate traffic). Partitioning is normalmente combined con replication: each particiГіn has varias rГ©plicas on diferente nodos.

sections.heading: Particionamiento por rango de claves
sections.content:
Assign a continuous rango of claves to each particiГіn (like volumes of an encyclopedia). 
Partition 1: delivery_id 1 - 100,000
Partition 2: delivery_id 100,001 - 200,000
Partition 3: delivery_id 200,001 - 300,000

Or by date:
Partition Jan-2026: events donde timestamp in January
Partition Feb-2026: events donde timestamp in February
Ventajas: вҖў Efficient rango escaneos (all datos for a rango is on one particiГіn) вҖў Easy to understand boundaries Desventajas: вҖў Hot spots: if escrituras have a natural ordering (timestamps), all escrituras go to the LATEST particiГіn while others sit idle вҖў Need manual or automatic rebalancing as datos grows Mitigation for time-series hot spots: 
-- Instead of partitioning by timestamp alone:
-- Prefix con sensor_id or region вҶ’ spreads escrituras
Partition clave = sensor_name + date
-- sensor_A_2026-07-21, sensor_B_2026-07-21 вҶ’ diferente particiones
Used by: HBase, BigTable, RethinkDB, MongoDB (con ranged shard clave)

sections.heading: Particionamiento por hash de la clave
sections.content:
Apply a hash function to the clave, assign hash ranges to particiones. Eliminates hot spots desde ordered claves. 
hash(clave) вҶ’ 0 to 2^32
Partition A: hash 0 - 1,073,741,823
Partition B: hash 1,073,741,824 - 2,147,483,647
Partition C: hash 2,147,483,648 - 3,221,225,471
Partition D: hash 3,221,225,472 - 4,294,967,295
Ventajas: вҖў Even distribution regardless of clave patterns вҖў No hot spots (even sequential claves get spread out) Desventajas: вҖў Loses rango consulta efficiency: adjacent claves end up on diferente particiones вҖў Range escaneos require querying ALL particiones (scatter/gather) Compromise: compound particiГіn clave (Cassandra) 
-- First part hashed (for distribution), rest ordenado within particiГіn
PRIMARY KEY ((driver_id), delivery_date, delivery_id)
-- Todas deliveries for a driver on mismo particiГіn (hashed by driver_id)
-- Within that particiГіn, ordenado by date вҶ’ efficient rango escaneo por driver
Used by: Cassandra, DynamoDB, Riak, Voldemort

sections.heading: ГҚndices secundarios con particionamiento
sections.content:
Partitioning gets complicated cuando you necesitar secondary Гӯndices (not just bГәsqueda by primary clave). Approach 1: Local Гӯndice (document-partitioned) Each particiГіn maintains its own secondary Гӯndice covering only its datos. 
Partition 0:                 Partition 1:
deliveries id 0-499         deliveries id 500-999
local Гӯndice: status         local Гӯndice: status
  PLANNED вҶ’ [1,7,42]        PLANNED вҶ’ [501,622]
  DELIVERED вҶ’ [3,9]          DELIVERED вҶ’ [503,700]
вҖў Write: update only local Гӯndice (rГЎpido) вҖў Read by secondary Гӯndice: scatter/gather вҖ” consulta ALL particiones, merge results вҖў Used by: MongoDB, Riak, Cassandra, Elasticsearch, Solr Approach 2: Global Гӯndice (term-partitioned) The Гӯndice itself is partitioned вҖ” by the indexed term, not by document. 
Index particiГіn A (status A-M):    Index particiГіn B (status N-Z):
  DELIVERED вҶ’ [3,503,700]           PLANNED вҶ’ [1,7,42,501,622]
вҖў Read: go to one Гӯndice particiГіn (rГЎpido lecturas) вҖў Write: puede necesitar to update varias Гӯndice particiones (slower, often async) вҖў Used by: DynamoDB global secondary Гӯndices, Riak search Idea clave para la entrevista: "There's siempre a trade-off. Local Гӯndices make escrituras rГЎpido but lecturas costoso. Global Гӯndices make lecturas rГЎpido but escrituras require distributed coordination."

sections.heading: Reequilibrado вҖ” quГ© ocurre cuando se mueven las particiones
sections.content: Triggers for rebalancing: вҖў Add a nuevo nodo (scale out) вҖў Remove a failed nodo вҖў Data grows unevenly Strategies: 1. Fixed number of particiones: вҖў Create muchos mГЎs particiones than nodos (e.g., 1000 particiones for 10 nodos вҶ’ 100 each) вҖў When adding nodo: steal some particiones desde others вҖў Partition boundaries don't change, only assignment to nodos вҖў Used by: Riak, Elasticsearch, CouchBase, Voldemort 2. Dynamic partitioning: вҖў Split particiГіn cuando it gets too grande, merge cuando too pequeГұo вҖў Number of particiones adapts to datos volume вҖў Used by: HBase, RethinkDB, MongoDB 3. Proportional to nodos: вҖў Fixed number of particiones por nodo вҖў When nuevo nodo joins: randomly split existing particiones вҖў Used by: Cassandra вҡ пёҸ Automatic vs manual rebalancing: Kleppmann warns: fully automatic rebalancing puede be dangerous. If a nodo is lento (not dead), automatic rebalancing puede cause cascading failures. Many producciГіn systems require human confirmation.

sections.heading: Enrutamiento de solicitudes вҖ” cГіmo encuentran los clientes la particiГіn correcta
sections.content:
Three approaches:

1. Client contacts ANY nodo вҶ’ nodo forwards to correcto particiГіn
   [Client] вҶ’ [Node 2] вҶ’ [Node 5 has particiГіn]

2. Client contacts enrutamiento tier (particiГіn-aware load balancer)
   [Client] вҶ’ [Router] вҶ’ [Node 5 has particiГіn]

3. Client is particiГіn-aware (knows the mapeo)
   [Client] в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҖвҶ’ [Node 5 has particiГіn]
How does anyone know which particiГіn lives donde? вҖў ZooKeeper/etcd: centralized metadata store (utilizado by HBase, Kafka, SolrCloud) вҖў Gossip protocol: nodos share particiГіn ownership among themselves (Cassandra) вҖў Config file: manual particiГіn mapeo (sencillo but static) PostgreSQL partitioning (built-in since v10): 
-- Declarative partitioning вҖ” no external coordinator necesario
CREATE TABLE delivery_events (
    id BIGINT,
    event_time TIMESTAMPTZ,
    event_type TEXT
) PARTITION BY RANGE (event_time);

CREATE TABLE events_2026_q1 PARTITION OF delivery_events
    FOR VALUES FROM ('2026-01-01') TO ('2026-04-01');
CREATE TABLE events_2026_q2 PARTITION OF delivery_events
    FOR VALUES FROM ('2026-04-01') TO ('2026-07-01');
-- Query planner automГЎticamente prunes irrelevant particiones
REWE aplicaciГіn: вҖў delivery_events: partitioned by month (most consultas have date rango) вҖў GPS telemetry: partitioned by driver_id hash (even distribution) вҖў Historical orders: particiГіn by year, mover antiguo particiones to cold almacenamiento

title: AnГЎlisis profundo del aislamiento de transacciones вҖ” MVCC, escritura skew y serializaciГіn

sections.heading: MГЎs allГЎ de los libros: quГ© revela DDIA sobre el aislamiento
sections.content: The standard aislamiento levels (READ COMMITTED, REPEATABLE READ, SERIALIZABLE) hide enormous complexity. DDIA exposes quГ© really happens: Idea clave: "There are big differences in the guarantees that databases actually provide, despite being ostensibly standardized. Nobody really knows quГ© 'repeatable leer' means." What each level actually prevents: | Anomaly | Read Committed | Snapshot/RR | Serializable | |---|---|---|---| | Dirty lecturas | вң“ prevented | вң“ | вң“ | | Dirty escrituras | вң“ prevented | вң“ | вң“ | | Read skew | вң— possible | вң“ prevented | вң“ | | Lost updates | вң— possible | вҡ пёҸ depends on DB | вң“ | | Write skew | вң— possible | вң— possible | вң“ prevented | | Phantoms | вң— possible | вң— possible | вң“ prevented | вҡ пёҸ MySQL/InnoDB does NOT detect lost updates bajo repeatable leer. PostgreSQL does. This matters in producciГіn.

sections.heading: MVCC вҖ” cГіmo funciona realmente el aislamiento por instantГЎnea
sections.content:
Multi-Version Concurrency Control вҖ” the mechanism behind PostgreSQL's REPEATABLE READ: 
Key principle: readers nunca block writers, writers nunca block readers.

Implementation (PostgreSQL):
вҖў Each transacciГіn gets a unique, increasing txid
вҖў Each fila has created_by (txid) and deleted_by (txid) fields
вҖў UPDATE = mark antiguo fila deleted + insert nuevo fila
вҖў Each transacciГіn only sees filas that were:
  1. Created by an already-committed transacciГіn (txid < mine)
  2. NOT deleted, or deleted by a not-yet-committed transacciГіn
Visibility rules (the 4 condiciones desde DDIA): 1. Ignore escrituras desde transacciones still in progress cuando mine started 2. Ignore escrituras desde aborted transacciones 3. Ignore escrituras desde transacciones con higher txid (started despuГ©s me) 4. Todas other escrituras are visible 
-- Practical ejemplo:
-- TX 12 starts, sees instantГЎnea at that moment
-- TX 13 transfers $100 desde account A to B
-- TX 12 still sees the OLD balances (consistente instantГЎnea)
-- Even despuГ©s TX 13 commits, TX 12's instantГЎnea doesn't change!
Consequences: вҖў Long-running consultas see a consistente point-in-time view вҖў VACUUM debe clean up antiguo fila versions (dead tuples) вҖў Table bloat if VACUUM puede't keep up con updates

sections.heading: Write skew вҖ” la anomalГӯa que el aislamiento por instantГЎnea no evita
sections.content:
The most dangerous anomaly that muchos developers don't know about: 
-- Hospital on-call ejemplo (desde DDIA):
-- Rule: at least 1 doctor debe be on-call at all times
-- Currently: Alice and Bob are both on-call

-- TX1 (Alice): "Am I the only one on-call? No, Bob is too. I puede leave."
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM doctors WHERE on_call = true AND shift_id = 123;
-- Returns 2 вҶ’ safe to remove myself
UPDATE doctors SET on_call = false WHERE id = 'alice' AND shift_id = 123;

-- TX2 (Bob): runs the SAME logic at the mismo time
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM doctors WHERE on_call = true AND shift_id = 123;
-- Also devuelve 2 (instantГЎnea!) вҶ’ safe to remove myself
UPDATE doctors SET on_call = false WHERE id = 'bob' AND shift_id = 123;

-- BOTH commit successfully. Now ZERO doctors are on-call! рҹ’Җ
Pattern: 1. SELECT checks a condiciГіn 2. Based on result, aplicaciГіn decides to proceed 3. UPDATE/INSERT changes something that would invalidate the condiciГіn 4. Another transacciГіn does the mismo on a DIFFERENT fila More ejemplos of escritura skew: вҖў Booking a meeting room (double-booking) вҖў Claiming a unique username (two users register mismo name) вҖў Preventing overdraft (two withdrawals check mismo balance) вҖў Seat reservation on a flight Why instantГЎnea aislamiento doesn't help: Each transacciГіn lecturas a consistente instantГЎnea and modifies DIFFERENT filas. No conflicto is detected porque they don't escritura to the mismo fila.

sections.heading: Fantasmas вҖ” el problema oculto
sections.content:
Write skew involves a phantom: a fila that one transacciГіn's leer would have seen if another transacciГіn's escritura had happened primero. 
-- Phantom ejemplo:
-- TX1: SELECT * FROM bookings WHERE room = 'A' AND time = '14:00';
-- Returns 0 filas вҶ’ room is free вҶ’ INSERT booking

-- TX2: Same consulta, mismo time, tambiГ©n devuelve 0 вҶ’ INSERT booking
-- Result: DOUBLE BOOKING (phantom fila didn't exist cuando checked)
The problema: You puede't use SELECT FOR UPDATE porque there's nothing to bloqueo вҖ” the fila doesn't exist yet! Solutions: 1. Materializing conflictos: 
-- Create a tabla of time slots explicitly
CREATE TABLE room_slots (
    room_id INT, time_slot TIMESTAMPTZ, PRIMARY KEY (room_id, time_slot)
);
-- Pre-populate all possible slots
-- Now you CAN bloqueo a fila:
SELECT * FROM room_slots
WHERE room_id = 'A' AND time_slot = '14:00'
FOR UPDATE;
-- If another TX tries to bloqueo the mismo slot вҶ’ blocks
2. Use SERIALIZABLE aislamiento: 
SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL SERIALIZABLE;
-- PostgreSQL will detect the conflicto and abort one transacciГіn
-- Application debe reintentar the aborted transacciГіn
3. Application-level constraints: UNIQUE constraints, advisory bloqueos, explicit coordination.

sections.heading: Aislamiento serializable вҖ” tres implementaciones
sections.content:
The strongest aislamiento level. Three approaches exist: 1. Actual Serial Execution (single-threaded) вҖў Execute ALL transacciones one at a time, on a single CPU core вҖў Surprisingly viable if: transacciones are short, datos fits in RAM вҖў Used by: VoltDB, Redis, Datomic вҖў Limitation: throughput limited to one core 2. Two-Phase Locking (2PL) вҖў Locks for lecturas AND escrituras. Readers block writers, writers block readers. вҖў "If TX A leer something, TX B puede't escritura it (until A commits)" вҖў Prevents ALL anomalies but massively reduces concurrency вҖў Used by: MySQL/InnoDB SERIALIZABLE, SQL Server (partially), DB2 вҖў Problem: deadlocks, rendimiento degradation (up to 100x slower) 
-- 2PL in action:
TX1: SELECT * FROM accounts WHERE id = 1; -- acquires shared bloqueo
